title: Mortality from short-term exposure to particulate matter, nitrogen dioxide and ozone across Europe
data_sources: QML air pollution model (Chen et al. 2024)

data_scope.spatial_coverage: Europe — 653 NUTS sub-national regions across 31 countries
data_scope.mortality_data: EARLY-ADAPT; restricted; available on request from national statistical agencies
data_scope.exposure_model: QML gridded daily Europe (Chen et al. Nat. Commun. 2024; doi:10.1038/s41467-024-46103-3)

core_conclusion: Using daily mortality and air pollution exposure data from 653 regions across 31 European countries (2003–2019), short-term exposure to PM2.5, coarse particulates, NO2, and ozone each raises mortality risk by 0.54–1.14% per 10 µg/m³. Their combined effect accounts for an estimated 146,517 annual premature deaths (95% CI: 119,992–173,771). Vulnerability is strongly heterogeneous by sex and age, and uniform-risk models systematically underestimate burden in Eastern and Southeastern Europe.
why_it_matters: First continental multi-pollutant short-term burden estimate; exposes systematic underestimation bias of uniform-risk models in Eastern Europe; demographic granularity reveals sex- and age-specific vulnerability gradients; provides direct input for EU air quality policy and early-warning systems; burden code is open on GitHub.
why_nature: Continental-scale policy-grade finding with direct EU regulatory relevance; demographic heterogeneity as scientific contribution beyond standard burden accounting; methodological novelty (WQS + BLUP meta-regression); equity angle exposing Eastern European data justice gap.
added_value_to_my_research: MGWR/GWR as spatially explicit risk estimator replacing uniform BLUP pooling — extends GWR toolkit into time-series epidemiology
Satellite AOD + deep-learning downscaling to extend burden mapping beyond EARLY-ADAPT data-sharing constraints — applies geospatial foundation model fine-tuning
Healthcare accessibility (2SFCA/R2SFCA) as mediating moderator in meta-regression — decomposes Eastern European mortality excess into pollution vs. care-access deficit
SHAP-based explainable ML on 653 region-specific risk estimates to attribute heterogeneous vulnerability to spatial context features
Real-time early-warning pipeline coupling CAMS forecasts with paper's risk surfaces and spatiotemporal KDE for per-demographic mortality-risk alerts
